Definition (EN): A viewpoint definition is a kind of requirement definition (see 7.21 ) that frames the concerns of one or more stakeholders regarding information about a modeled system or domain of interest.
Context/Example (EN): A viewpoint definition is a kind of requirement definition (see 7.21 ) that frames the concerns of one or more stakeholders regarding information about a modeled system or domain of interest.

Definition (EN): A viewpoint usage is a requirement usage that is a usage of a viewpoint definition.
Context/Example (EN): A viewpoint usage is a requirement usage that is a usage of a viewpoint definition.

Definition (EN): A view definition is a kind of part definition (see 7.11 ) that specifies how to create a view artifact to satisfy one or more viewpoints.
Context/Example (EN): A view definition is a kind of part definition (see 7.11 ) that specifies how to create a view artifact to satisfy one or more viewpoints.

Definition (EN): A view artifact is a rendering of information that addresses some aspect of a system or domain of interest of concern to one or more stakeholders.
Context/Example (EN): A view artifact is a rendering of information that addresses some aspect of a system or domain of interest of concern to one or more stakeholders.

Definition (EN): A view definition can include view conditions to extract the relevant model content, and a rendering that specifies how the model content should be rendered in a view artifact.
Context/Example (EN): A view condition is specified using metadata, in the same way as for a filter condition on a package (see 7.5 ).

Definition (EN): A view definition can include view conditions to extract the relevant model content, and a rendering that specifies how the model content should be rendered in a view artifact.
Context/Example (EN): A view definition can include view conditions to extract the relevant model content, and a rendering that specifies how the model content should be rendered in a view artifact.

Definition (EN): A view usage is a kind of part usage (see 7.11 ) that is a usage of a view definition.
Context/Example (EN): A view usage is a kind of part usage (see 7.11 ) that is a usage of a view definition.

Definition (EN): A rendering definition is a kind of part definition (see 7.11 ) that specifies how a view artifact is to be rendered.
Context/Example (EN): A rendering definition is a kind of part definition (see 7.11 ) that specifies how a view artifact is to be rendered.

Definition (EN): A rendering usage is a kind of part usage that is a usage of a rendering definition.
Context/Example (EN): A rendering usage is a kind of part usage that is a usage of a rendering definition.

Definition (EN): A compartment is a view usage (see 7.26.2 ).
Context/Example (EN): A compartment is a view usage (see 7.26.2 ).

Definition (EN): A diagram is also a view usage.
Context/Example (EN): A diagram is also a view usage.

Definition (EN): Expose relationships are declared like import relationships (see 7.5.3 ), but using the keyword expose instead of import and with no specification of visibility.
Context/Example (EN): In addition, a view usage can specify which elements are actually to be exposed by the view. This is done using expose relationships, which are a special kind of import relationships.

Definition (EN): This package defines normative standard view definitions that shall be supported by any tool conforming to the SysML graphical notation.
Context/Example (EN): The StandardViewDefinitions package in the Systems Model Library (see 9.2.20 ) provides a set of standard view definitions for typical kinds of diagrams, including the valid contents for the view.
